regimeSub: AI-driven equity melt-up meets sticky 4.2% CPI and a Warsh-led Fed threatening a hike, even as Iran ceasefire crushes oil.
themes: Iran ceasefire + US-Iran deal collapsed the oil premium (Brent -38% QoQ) — disinflation tailwind fights sticky core
New Fed Chair Warsh holds but dangles a September hike as CPI hits a 3-yr high — front-end priced for pain
AI/semiconductor rally powers SPX to 7499 and best quarter since 2020 — breadth thin, valuations stretched
Yen at 40-year low (162.83) as BoJ lags — carry trades funding the global equity bid, fragility building

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy 3.63% vs CPI 4.2% = negative real rate; Fed behind the curve, hike bias justified.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +28bp, mildly positive; hike risk flattens front-end, no recession signal from steepening.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Tight — risk-on melt-up and lower oil suppress default fears; complacent to hike shock.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 16.45, low; market shrugging off Fed hawkishness and geopolitics — vol is cheap insurance.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $73, down 38% in Q2 on Iran ceasefire; benchmark loading anomalies flag structural stress.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $3993 near highs despite risk-on; hedging fiscal/inflation debasement and negative real rates.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1556 weak KRW but KOSPI 8345 ripping on chip cycle; FX weakness tolerated amid tech boom.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: AI/chip rally is the market's engine — leadership narrow, any AI wobble hits everything.

headline: Oil-shock fears are dead — Iran's ceasefire tanked Brent 38%, but a 4.2% CPI print and a hawkish Warsh Fed now threaten to hike into a raging AI-led equity melt-up with the yen at 40-year lows.
